ogTitle: Abercorn vs Abingdon Downs: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Abercorn and Abingdon Downs.
heading: Abercorn vs Abingdon Downs — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Abercorn, Queensland and Abingdon Downs, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Abingdon Downs has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Abercorn (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Abercorn or Abingdon Downs safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Abercorn has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Abingdon Downs scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Abercorn scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Abingdon Downs scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Abercorn or Abingdon Downs?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Abingdon Downs has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
